title: Resolve merge conflicts for Feature Root: Fix intermittent test suite hang at end of pytest run

handoff_summary.completed: Updated 4 tests to use assertIn instead of assertEqual for result.blocked checks, and changed 1 index-based execution_history[-2] assertion to a search-based assertIn check. These tests were failing because when review/tester beads are blocked (via needs_changes verdict or compat path), the scheduler creates corrective developer beads in the same cycle. Without a feature_root_id (not present in unit test setup), the corrective is immediately blocked too, causing both the review/tester bead and the corrective bead to appear in result.blocked.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — all identified test assertion failures are fixed.
handoff_summary.risks: None introduced. The assertions are now consistent with the CLAUDE.md guidance and with the assertIn pattern already used elsewhere in the same test file (e.g., test_legacy_review_without_verdict_records_compat_warning, telemetry tests). The node-down messages in the test output (gw0/gw2 workers) are pre-existing infrastructure issues addressed by the earlier beads already merged into main.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry takt merge for feature/b-1ba2d8b2 — the test gate should now pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — tests only, no source code modified.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The fix applies assertIn rather than assertEqual for result.blocked checks in 4 tests. This is the CLAUDE.md-mandated approach when the same-cycle slot-fill loop can add additional items to result.blocked. An alternative would have been to remove the immediate corrective creation from finalize.py, but that would have changed production behavior that was intentionally added (corrective beads are supposed to be dispatched in the same cycle for responsiveness). The test-side fix is correct. The index-based execution_history[-2] assertion was changed to a search-based assertIn because same-cycle _reevaluate_blocked can append a corrective_planned event after the blocked event, shifting the index.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: N/A — this bead modified test assertions only, not executable production code. The tester agent should verify the full test suite passes after this fix.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The node-down messages (gw0/gw2 workers not properly terminated) visible in the original test output are a separate infrastructure issue previously worked on by beads B-5c4b11c3, B-b490fa1b, B-52b0a471, B-609cfa01, B-9afb6ff1. Those fixes are already merged into this branch. Any residual intermittent hangs are out of scope for this corrective bead.

conflict_risks: None — tests only, no source code modified.

execution_history.summary: Fixed 5 test assertions in test_scheduler_finalize.py that failed because same-cycle corrective bead dispatch added extra entries to result.blocked.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
